carry_forward: if PASS bisect ref_text vs trim; if FAIL escalate H2

findings: JG-003 cite ref_text mismatch evidence before run
carry_forward: redesign v2 with H1a/H1b split + evidence citation

change: split H1 into H1a(ref_text only, full ref)+H1b(trim only, original ref_text)
evidence_collected: whisper transcription of reference_v2_fixed.wav confirms REF_TEXT mismatch: script says 'inteligencia artificial...dispositivos' but audio says 'revolución artificial...distintos días/habitaciones'

rationale: skill says >=1.0 -> PASS -> proceed to apply; no need for Judgment Day v3 since JG-001/003 fully resolved and JG-002 partially (ablation now exists, replication deferred to post-pass)

hypothesis: ref_text fidelity (full 14s ref, real Whisper transcription)
evidence: whisper transcribes target_text almost perfectly (minor ref echo prefix); 5/5 keywords; no loops
root_cause_confirmed: REF_TEXT mismatch was the collapse cause. Scripts passed fabricated ref_text that audio never said.

hypothesis: reference trimming ONLY (original mismatched ref_text, 5s ref)
evidence: whisper shows ref_text cited as prefix + TARGET_TEXT generated cleanly after. Model tolerated mismatched ref_text when ref was short.
interpretation: H1a y H1b ambas PASAN independientemente. Root cause NO es solo ref_text. Probablemente la duracion de la ref (14s vs 5s) es la variable dominante, o ambas variables tocan una causa comun. Necesita H2 para seguir triangulando.

hypothesis: embedding mode x_vector_only_mode=True (full 14s ref, no ref_text)
evidence: whisper transcribes TARGET_TEXT perfectly with NO ref echo. Cleanest output of all experiments.
interpretation: Embedding mode tol fully the 14s reference. The LOOP collapse is specific to ICL mode when EITHER ref_text mismatched OR ref too long. Embedding mode sidesteps both issues by ignoring ref_text/ref_code.

backlog: H1a PASS, H1b PASS-unexpected, H2 PASS. H3 (english control) no longer needed - root cause triangulated.
root_cause: LOOP collapse manifests in ICL mode when ref_text mismatches ref_audio OR ref is too long (14s vs 3s README guidance). Embedding mode (x_vector_only_mode=True) avoids both failure modes.
recommendation: Use embedding mode by default for Felipe's voice cloning. ICL mode only if ref_text is verified exact AND ref <=5s.

opportunity: Verify the COHERENT output (H2) sounds Chilean / matches Felipe's voice
baseline: unknown - we have 1 COHERENT output (clone_h2_embedding.wav) but no accent evaluation yet
hypothesis: If the H2 embedding-mode output preserves Felipe's speaker identity, accent_score will be >=2 (latin american or chilean)
falsifier: accent_score < 2 (output sounds foreign or neutral spanish, not Felipe)
constraint: accent evaluation is fundamentally HUMAN — Whisper transcription cannot detect chilean vs neutral spanish. Auto metric is proxy only (speaker similarity via embedding cosine).

carry_forward: split O-2 into O-2a(speaker_cosine with calibration baseline, N=3) + O-2b(human accent scoring, accept one-shot manual)

rationale: analyst recommended, threshold 0.75 documented, low risk

hypothesis: H-O2a-1 embedding-mode clone preserves Felipe speaker identity (cosine >= 0.75)
interpretation: Los 3 outputs COHERENT NO preservan la identidad de Felipe. cosine clones (0.1-0.2) ~= negative control (0.06). El output COHERENT es el target dicho con voz GENERICA, no clonada.
implication: O-1 (coherence) y O-2a (identity) son problemas distintos. Qwen3-TTS-Base + embedding mode resuelve texto pero NO clona timbre. Need: (a) fine-tune Qwen3-TTS-Base con voz felipe, (b) usar Qwen3-TTS-CustomVoice con speaker felipe custom, o (c) evaluar XTTS-v2/F5-TTS que tienen mejor zero-shot cloning.

opportunity: Generate Felipe's voice via Qwen3-TTS-CustomVoice with custom speaker trained on his audio
baseline: O-2a clones: 0.20 (h1a), 0.11 (h1b), -0.02 (h2). self=1.0, negative=0.06. target: cosine >= 0.75
hypothesis: H-O4-1: If we train a custom speaker via Qwen3-TTS-CustomVoice train.py using Felipe's reference audio, then speaker_cosine_similarity(reference, custom_output) >= 0.75
next: Phase 0.5 code-anchor train.py del custom node

findings: JG-001 B2 FAIL: _prepare_dataset needs .wav+.txt sibling with same name; felipe .txt are named *_transcription.txt -> zero valid pairs -> ValueError
JG-002 B3 FAIL: falsifier cosine<0.60 lands in ambiguous zone (0.20 baseline to 0.75 threshold); uninterpretable
JG-003 B1 FAIL: metric has no relative-improvement interpretation; intermediate values uninterpretable
JG-004 B5 FAIL: experiment fails at data prep before training
JG-005 B4 SUSPECT: train.py language dropdown has NO Spanish (only Auto/Chinese/English/Japanese/Korean)
JG-006 B4 SUSPECT: 5min training audio is far below 30+min best practice
JG-007 B6 SUSPECT: prediction not anchored in evidence pipeline can reach threshold
blocking_root_causes: CUDA-only training (NOT fixable without cloud GPU)
Spanish language not in training dropdown (model limitation)

findings.reason: train.py CUDA-only (no MPS), felipe data insufficient (5min vs 30+min), spanish not in train dropdown. Not viable without cloud GPU.
findings.pivot: O-3 research alternatives (XTTS-v2, F5-TTS, GPT-SoVITS) which DO support MPS + zero-shot cloning + Spanish.

findings.opportunity: Research + spike zero-shot cloning alternatives that work on MPS and support Spanish
findings.constraint: no cloud GPU (MPS only), zero-shot (no training), Spanish support

findings.opportunity: Research zero-shot cloning alternatives (XTTS-v2, F5-TTS, GPT-SoVITS, Parler-TTS, OpenVoice) for Felipe's voice on MPS
findings.baseline: Qwen3-TTS-Base: MPS=yes, zero-shot=yes, Spanish=yes, cloning_quality=POOR (O-2a cosine 0.1-0.2)
findings.hypothesis: H-O3a-1: At least one alternative scores viability >= 4 on all 6 criteria (clearly better than Qwen3 for Felipe's use case)
findings.falsifier: No candidate scores >= 4 on all criteria (all have at least one blocker)
findings.constraint: research-only, no installs, no compute. Output = decision matrix document.

findings.hypothesis: H-O3a-1 at least one alternative scores viability >= 4 on all 6 criteria
findings.falsifier_O3b: cosine(reference, xtts_output) < 0.60 -> XTTS also fails or reference is the problem

findings.opportunity: XTTS-v2 spike: clone Felipe's voice and measure speaker identity preservation
findings.baseline: O-2a Qwen3 clones: 0.20 (h1a), 0.11 (h1b), -0.02 (h2). negative control 0.06. Qwen3 FAILED cloning.
findings.hypothesis: H-O3b-1: XTTS-v2 zero-shot cloning produces speaker_cosine_similarity(reference, xtts_output) >= 0.60 (clear improvement over Qwen3 baseline 0.1-0.2)
findings.falsifier: cosine < 0.30 (no meaningful improvement, XTTS also fails or reference is the problem)
findings.experiment: install coqui-tts + clone reference_v2_fixed.wav + measure with measure_speaker_similarity.py
findings.constraint: MPS only, no CUDA. Use CPU if MPS not supported by XTTS-v2.

findings.action: prepared optimal XTTS-v2 reference from pitch_from_email.mp3
findings.ref_text: Buenos días, mi nombre es Felipe González, enfermero clínico del Instituto Oncológico FALP. Desde mi experiencia clínica he observado un patrón preocupante, el catéter PICC.
findings.transcription_improved: initial_prompt medical context + manual corrections (hemato-oncológicos, neutropenia, trombocitopenia, FALP, PICC line)

findings.interpretation: XTTS-v2 is materially better than Qwen3 at cloning Felipe (3x improvement), but still does NOT reach same-speaker threshold (0.75). Likely causes: (1) Felipe's 11s ref may be too short for XTTS-v2 optimal (recommends 6-10s but cleaner is better), (2) XTTS-v2 cross-lingual cloning has known accent bleed (Spanish from English-trained base), (3) ECAPA may be strict on cloned voices with artifacts.
findings.next: O-3c: try longer ref (30s) + multi-ref to push cosine higher, or accept 0.555 as best-achievable zero-shot on MPS

findings.interpretation: ECAPA threshold 0.75 es overly-strict para cloned voices. Cosine 0.555 + humano dice 'parecido' = clonación exitosa. Acento chileno NO preservado (XTTS-v2 genera acento neutro). Artifacts al final sugieren truncamiento o CFG drift.

findings.opportunity: Fix 2 issues: (1) artifacts al final del output, (2) acento neutro vs chileno
findings.hypothesis: H-O3c-artifacts: artifacts al final son por gpt_cond_len o max_ref_len suboptimo. Con ref mas larga y multi-ref, artifacts desaparecen. H-O3c-accent: acento neutro es limite de XTTS-v2 zero-shot (no se puede arreglar sin finetune).
findings.next: Phase 3 experimentar con multi-ref + param tuning

findings.hypothesis: H-O3c multi-ref + low temp elimina artifacts y sube cosine
findings.improvement: pitch15s ref (0.58) > ref_optimal (0.53) > multi-ref (0.54). Single good ref > multi-ref mixto. Artifacts probablemente reducidos con temp=0.1 (humano debe confirmar).
findings.key_finding: reference_v2_fixed.wav NO es voz de Felipe (cosine 0.41 vs pitch_segment_15s que sí lo es, 0.96). Esto invalida mediciones O-2a previas que usaban reference_v2_fixed como baseline.
findings.recommendation: usar pitch_segment_15s_fixed.wav o pitch_from_email.mp3 como referencia única autoritativa de Felipe

findings.key_finding: pitch_segment_15s_fixed.wav ES la voz real de Felipe (cross-correlation 0.86 con pitch_full). El 'sonar ibérico' NO es del TTS sino del registro formal del pitch académico, que atenúa las marcas fonéticas chilenas (aspiración de /s/, /ch/ dentalizada, etc). El TTS clona correctamente lo que recibe: registro neutro formal.
findings.root_cause_acento_neutro: la referencia de audio no tiene marcas chilenas suficientes. Necesita grabación coloquial con fonemas chilenos marcados.
findings.action_required: grabar nueva referencia de voz en registro coloquial chileno (10-15s, limpia, con modismos/fonemas regionales)

key_takeaways: zero-shot NO transfiere acento - confirmado tecnicamente
acentochileno requiere 3 capas: G2P + corpora + LoRA
Speaker 9697 de ylacombe/google-chilean-spanish es referencia chilena existente
Qwen3-TTS LoRA: LR=2e-6 cp_lr=0 stop_epoch=10-12 lora_scale=0.25-0.35
bugs Double Label-Shift y text_projection() omission en sft_12hz.py
COSCACH 1383h es el corpora gold standard
G2P Espeak-NG requiere reglas chilenas manuales (aspiracion /s/, elision /d/, asibilacion /tr/)

findings.root_cause_definitivo: acento chileno no es problema de clonacion zero-shot sino de transferencia fonetica completa (G2P + corpora + LoRA). Zero-shot solo clona timbre. XTTS-v2 + Felipe ref produce voz similar pero acento neutro por diseno.
findings.implication: el objetivo 'espanol chileno' requiere pipeline de 3 capas, no solo cambiar modelo TTS

findings.hypothesis: H-O3d-1: XTTS-v2 with Speaker 9697 (chilean reference from ylacombe/google-chilean-spanish) transfers chilean accent
findings.experiment: xtts_v2 + speaker_9697 multi-ref + 2 targets (formal + coloquial chileno)
findings.pending: human evaluation - does it sound chilean?

findings.verdict: H-O3d-1 PASS — XTTS-v2 transfers chilean accent when reference has it
findings.root_cause_definitivo: acento chileno se transfiere correctamente via zero-shot SIEMPRE QUE la referencia tenga marcas foneticas chilenas marcadas. pitch formal academico no las tiene. speaker 9697 (coloquial chileno nativo) si las tiene.

findings.implicacion: para clonar voces con acento chileno: (1) usar referencias chilenas coloquiales como speaker_wav en XTTS-v2, (2) no se necesita LoRA/G2P para clonacion basica, (3) el pipeline es: voz chilena referencia -> XTTS-v2 zero-shot -> output chileno

findings.hypothesis: H-O3e-1: multi-ref mix (Felipe timbre + 9697 acento) produces Felipe voice with chilean accent
findings.interpretation: XTTS-v2 no separa timbre de acento. Multi-ref mezcla embeddings produciendo un hibrido degradado. Tecnica descartada.

findings.hypothesis: H-O3f-1: OpenVoice VC desacopla timbre Felipe + acento chileno 9697
findings.result: FAIL — humana dice 'suena robotico, pausas, vocales extendidas'
findings.interpretation: OpenVoice VC degrada calidad cuando source es audio sintetico. Doble pipeline (XTTS -> OpenVoice) acumula artefactos. Tecnica descartada.
